title: From Wavefunction Collapse to Superconductivity: Evolution of the Electronic State in Compressed GaNb4Se8
authors: Yuejian Wang, Zhongyan Wu, K C Bhupendra, Dongzhou Zhang, Lin Wang, Sanjay V. Khare, Lilian Prodan, Vladimir Tsurkan
abstract: Understanding how electronic transport evolves from localized to itinerant regimes in correlated cluster solids remains an important challenge in condensed-matter physics. Here we investigate the pressure-dependent transport properties of the lacunar spinel GaNb4Se8, a cluster Mott insulator at ambient conditions. At low pressures, the resistivity follows Efros-Shklovskii variable-range hopping, indicating Coulomb-gap-controlled carrier localization (x ~ 6.1 Angstrom). A crossover toward metallic transport begins near ~ 5 GPa, whereas a crystallographic transition from the cubic phase to a monoclinic C2 phase occurs at significantly higher pressure (~ 20 GPa), establishing a hierarchy characterized by the decoupling of electronic delocalization from structural symmetry change. At higher pressures, superconductivity (xi(0) ~ 80-90 Angstrom) emerges from the correlated metallic regime. These results identify GaNb4Se8 as a platform for studying correlation-controlled transport evolution in cluster-based solids.
summary: This paper investigates how the electronic state of the cluster Mott insulator GaNb4Se8 changes under pressure. It finds that the material transitions from a localized regime to a metallic regime at 5 GPa, and eventually becomes superconducting at higher pressures. Notably, this electronic evolution occurs independently of the structural transition to a monoclinic phase, which only happens at 20 GPa. This discovery highlights GaNb4Se8 as a key platform for studying correlation-controlled transport in cluster-based solids.
main_problem: Understanding the evolution of electronic transport from localized to itinerant regimes in correlated cluster solids under pressure.
main_result: The study reveals a decoupling of electronic delocalization from structural changes, where metallic transport begins at 5 GPa and superconductivity emerges at higher pressures, well before the structural transition to a monoclinic phase at 20 GPa.
method: Pressure-dependent transport property measurements on the lacunar spinel GaNb4Se8.
model_or_system: The system is the lacunar spinel GaNb4Se8, which acts as a cluster Mott insulator at ambient conditions.
key_observables: Electrical resistivity, crystallographic phase (cubic to monoclinic), and superconducting coherence length (xi(0)).
important_parameters: Pressure (ranging from low pressure to 20 GPa), localization length (x ~ 6.1 Angstrom), and superconducting coherence length (xi(0) ~ 80-90 Angstrom).
paper_structure: The paper investigates the pressure-dependent transport properties of GaNb4Se8, moving from the localized Efros-Shklovskii regime to a metallic crossover and finally to a superconducting state, while comparing these electronic changes to structural phase transitions.
